Deny-all tables (RLS enabled, no policy → fail-closed, accessible only via the service-role client which bypasses RLS): store_ticket_counters (1386), supplier_sla_acceptances (1434), supplier_verification_docs (1436), daily_briefings, ratings, technicians, ticket_disputes, ticket_dispute_messages, ticket_suppliers, whatsapp_sessions, ticket_chat_messages, ticket_chat_reads. All 68 public tables have RLS enabled — none is missing it. Every SECURITY DEFINER function pins search_path (admin_db_stats -> 'public','pg_catalog'; the rest -> 'public') and the sensitive/RPC-reachable ones correctly REVOKE EXECUTE from anon/authenticated and grant only to service_role: admin_db_stats (1089-1091), reserve_upload_quota (1114-1116), append_session_photo / handle_new_user / assign_store_job_ref (1310-1312). handle_new_user() clamps self-signup to role='individual' (1272-1273). The app_* RLS helper functions are intentionally EXECUTE-able (called per-query by policies) and only read via auth.uid().
findings.whyItMatters: Documents that the deny-all surface and the SECURITY DEFINER surface are sound, so remediation effort can focus on the RLS policy gaps above. One minor defence-in-depth note: the two audit trigger functions log_ticket_event() (1315-1327) and archive_ticket_notifications() (1330-1339) are SECURITY DEFINER but not EXECUTE-revoked from anon/authenticated; they return `trigger` and reference NEW/OLD so are not meaningfully invokable via PostgREST RPC, hence not a practical risk.
findings.evidence: schema.sql:1068 admin_db_stats search_path; 1089-1091 revoke/grant; 1310-1312 EXECUTE revokes; 1272-1273 signup role clamp; deny-all comments 1386/1434/1436, 1810-1813, 2033-2039.
findings.rootCause: N/A — confirmation of good posture.
findings.requiredSecureOutcome: Maintain fail-closed deny-all + pinned search_path on all SECURITY DEFINER functions.
findings.fixApproach: Optional hardening only: also revoke EXECUTE on log_ticket_event() and archive_ticket_notifications() from anon/authenticated to match the other trigger functions.
testsRequired: Anon/authenticated SELECT on each deny-all table -> 0 rows

findings.title: CONTROL CONFIRMED: project progress is server-computed and not client-settable
findings.lines: schema.sql:1871-1876 (generated column) + 1943-1950 (RLS admin-only write); stores/[storeId]/route.ts:36-66; guard.ts:15-26; progress.ts:46-63
findings.problem: No defect — verification that the 25%×4 model is derived server-side from stored workflow/evidence state, and that no store_manager or regional_manager can set progress, milestone timestamps, or completion directly (client input is never trusted for these values).
findings.whyItMatters: Directly answers audit item (1): the progress model is trustworthy and tamper-resistant at both the route and DB layers.
findings.evidence: project_stores.progress_percentage is `integer generated always as (…25 per non-null milestone timestamp…) stored`. Milestone timestamps are set only by the milestone action, which runs projectAdminAuth (role must be 'system_admin', guard.ts:23) and writes [col]=now (server time), and requires uploaded evidence for before/after/signoff milestones (route :42-48). RLS project_stores write = system_admin only; RM/executive get read-only. Overall % = average of store % over the derived store count (progress.ts projectProgress).
findings.fixApproach: No action required. (Optional nicety: milestones can be marked in any order, but since % is a count×25 this does not misstate progress.)

findings.title: CONTROL CONFIRMED: separation of duties holds in the workflow engine (supplier cannot sign off own work or resolve own dispute)
findings.lines: workflow.ts:141-171 (approve/close_out roles); dispute/route.ts:248-253 (confirm requires other party), :224-233 (withdraw/retract role-gated); signoff/route.ts:18 (RM-only)
findings.problem: No defect at the engine layer — this documents that audit item (3) passes for the /transition and /dispute routes. NOTE: this engine-level control is undermined at the DB layer by the tickets/signoffs RLS write policies (see the critical/high RLS findings), which is where the actual exposure lies.
findings.whyItMatters: Confirms the workflow logic enforces separation of duties; the residual risk is purely the RLS bypass captured separately.
findings.evidence: In lib/workflow TRANSITIONS, 'approve' (COC/POC) and 'close_out' are roles ['regional_manager','executive','individual'] — supplier is absent; the supplier's only outbound moves are submit/accept actions. In /dispute, 'confirm' rejects when openDispute.pending_by === actingRole (the OTHER party must confirm), and withdraw (supplier-only, concedes → upheld) / retract (RM-only, drops → withdrawn) are role-restricted, so a supplier cannot unilaterally drop the RM's snag/evidence request in their own favour. The /signoff review endpoint is regional_manager-only.
findings.fixApproach: No action on the engine; remediate via the RLS findings.
findings.scoreImpact: None — positive assurance (conditional on fixing RLS).

findings.title: audit_logs / ticket_events are immutable to end users, but there is no DB-level append-only/tamper-evidence guard
findings.problem: Immutability holds for end users (correct), but it rests entirely on the absence of a write policy. The service-role client and any future system_admin write policy can UPDATE/DELETE audit rows freely; there is no append-only trigger or hash-chain making tampering detectable.
findings.whyItMatters: For a maintenance/quoting platform the audit trail may be evidentiary. The current posture is adequate against tenant users but offers no protection or detection against privileged (service-role) rewriting of history.
findings.evidence: audit_logs has RLS enabled (1405) and only a SELECT policy 'audit read' gated on app_is_company_wide() (1467-1469) — no INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE policy, so RLS-bound users cannot write or alter rows. ticket_events is the same (read-only policy at 1747, writes only via the SECURITY DEFINER trigger log_ticket_event). Ordinary users therefore CANNOT alter the audit trail.
findings.fixApproach: Accept as-is for now (end-user immutability is met). If tamper-evidence is desired later, add a BEFORE UPDATE/DELETE trigger raising an exception on audit_logs/ticket_events, or a periodic hash-chain. No change required to ship.

findings.title: Secrets hygiene, security headers, keystore, and service worker checks passed
findings.problem: Positive verifications for the audit scope (no action needed): (1) No hardcoded secrets/tokens found in app/lib/components/scripts; all `NEXT_PUBLIC_*` reads are legitimately public (SUPABASE_URL/ANON_KEY, SENTRY_DSN, VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY, APP_URL). (2) No `.env`/`.env.local` tracked — only `.env.example`. (3) Release signing is clean: `*.keystore`, `*.jks`, `android/keystore.properties` are gitignored; only `keystore.properties.example` (placeholders `CHANGE_ME`) is tracked; build.gradle loads creds from the gitignored file. (4) Security headers are strong: HSTS w/ preload+includeSubDomains, X-Content-Type-Options nosniff, X-Frame-Options DENY, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy; per-request nonce CSP with `strict-dynamic` and no script `unsafe-inline` (unsafe-eval dev-only), plus frame-ancestors 'none', object-src 'none', base-uri/form-action 'self'. (5) sw.js has NO fetch/cache handler — push + notificationclick only — so there is no stale-bundle risk and no offline caching of sensitive data. (6) WhatsApp webhook verifies x-hub-signature-256 fail-closed in production and uses constant-time compares; cron routes fail-closed (CRON_SECRET bearer OR authenticated executive/system_admin).
findings.whyItMatters: Documents what was checked and found sound so the parent review knows these areas were covered, not skipped.
findings.evidence: git ls-files shows no env/keystore secrets tracked; next.config.mjs:41-45 headers; proxy.ts:13 script-src nonce+strict-dynamic; sw.js has no `fetch` listener; whatsapp route.ts:411-423 fail-closed signature verify.
findings.rootCause: N/A — verification note.
findings.requiredSecureOutcome: N/A
findings.fixApproach: No action required; retain these controls.

endpoints.roles: Any authenticated; branches by profile: individual (standalone), store_manager/client (must have store_users link + company_id else 403). Other roles fall through to the store branch and 403 without a store link.
endpoints.tenantChecks: Derives company_id/store_id/region_id server-side from the caller's own profile + store_users link; store must be open. No client-supplied tenant fields trusted.
endpoints.inputValidation: zod BodySchema; description required; title auto-composed; operational_impact/severity/priority derived server-side (not from raw client priority).
endpoints.sensitiveOutput: Returns the created ticket row (own tenant).

endpoints.roles: SM owner (creator or store_users-linked), the ticket's RM (rmOwnsTicket), executive, system_admin. Managers may set P1–P4 priority directly.
endpoints.tenantChecks: Explicit: individual → created_by===user; else prof.company_id must equal ticket.company_id, then ownership/role gate. Update runs on admin (RLS-bypass) client after checks.
endpoints.inputValidation: zod PatchSchema; title+description required; impact/severity/flags derived; priority clamped to P1–P4 for managers.

endpoints.roles: SM owner or individual owner only (creator or store_users link).
endpoints.tenantChecks: individual → created_by===user; else company_id match + ownership. Only status 'open' deletable.

endpoints.roles: Per-action via lib/workflow resolveTransition(status,action,role): store_manager/regional_manager/supplier/executive/individual as listed per status. Company-scoped access via hasAccess().
endpoints.tenantChecks: company_id compare for non-supplier roles; supplier gated by hasAccess() (supplier_users + ticket_suppliers). SEE FINDING: hasAccess treats any ticket_suppliers row (any status) as access. supplierId written unvalidated.
endpoints.inputValidation: zod BodySchema (loose: amount z.any(); each case validates its own fields; amounts via Number()>0). Updates built server-side, no body spread.

endpoints.tenantChecks: ticket.company_id===me.company_id AND rmOwnsTicket.
endpoints.rateLimit: None (read; generates signed URLs, RM+region scoped).
endpoints.sensitiveOutput: Quote amounts + signed private attachment URLs + ticket detail/photos — all region-scoped.

endpoints.roles: supplier only (403); must hold an active (not awarded/closed/declined) ticket_suppliers invite.
endpoints.tenantChecks: Access by invite, not company (pool suppliers cross-company by design); invite existence is the gate; supplier_id taken from the matched invite, not body.
individual → created_by===user; else company_id match + (RM → rmOwnsTicket). quoteId constrained with .eq('ticket_id',ticket.id). Idempotency guard on non-pending quotes (409).
company_id match + rmOwnsTicket.

endpoints.roles: supplier (awarded-supplier users), individual owner, regional_manager (rmOwnsTicket), executive (company match). POST actions further gated (only supplier can raise/withdraw; only RM can retract).

policies.issue: WITH CHECK is only (company_id = app_company_id()); it drops the USING role/region gate (company-wide OR regional_manager-in-region). INSERT (checks only WITH CHECK) is therefore open to ANY company member incl. store_manager/supplier, and UPDATE new-row can retarget approval.ticket_id to a ticket outside the actor's region.
Symmetric USING/WITH CHECK = (id = app_company_id()) AND role = system_admin. Correctly gated on both sides. No issue.

WITH CHECK = (company_id = app_company_id()) only; drops the app_is_company_wide() gate in USING. INSERT is open to any company member (store_manager/supplier), though only exec/admin may read/update. Lower-priv users can forge decision_items.

No explicit WITH CHECK, but Postgres defaults it to the USING expr (user_id = auth.uid()), which DOES prevent user_id reassignment to another user. Benign; only a user's own notifications are writable. Recommend adding an explicit WITH CHECK for clarity.
Symmetric USING/WITH CHECK = (user_id = auth.uid()). Correctly self-scoped. No issue.

No WITH CHECK; defaults to USING = (company_id = app_company_id()) AND app_can_see_ticket(ticket_id). app_can_see_ticket() returns true for STORE users whose store owns the ticket, so a store_manager (the client) can PATCH the supplier's quote row incl. amount / amount_incl_vat / status directly via PostgREST. Client-side tampering with quote pricing/acceptance.

WITH CHECK = (company_id = app_company_id()) AND app_can_see_ticket(ticket_id). Any company member who can see the ticket (incl. store_manager) can INSERT quotes — not just suppliers — enabling forged quotes.
Symmetric USING/WITH CHECK = (company_id = app_company_id()) AND role = system_admin. Correctly gated. No issue.
WITH CHECK = (company_id = app_company_id()) only, no role gate. Any company member can insert export-metadata rows. Low impact (metadata only).
WITH CHECK = (company_id = app_company_id()) only, no role gate. Any company member can insert report rows. Low impact.

WITH CHECK = (company_id = app_company_id()) only; drops app_can_see_ticket(ticket_id) present in USING. INSERT is open to any company member for ANY ticket in the company (even ones they cannot see), and UPDATE new-row can retarget ticket_id. Lets a low-priv user forge an RM completion sign-off.
Symmetric USING/WITH CHECK = company + role IN (system_admin, executive). Correctly gated. No issue.

WITH CHECK = (company_id = app_company_id()) only; drops the (company-wide OR regional_manager) role gate in USING. INSERT is open to any company member (store_manager/supplier), and UPDATE new-row only re-checks company_id. Low-priv users can forge/retarget snags.

Symmetric USING/WITH CHECK = company + role IN (system_admin, regional_manager). Role-gated on both sides, but a regional_manager can create/modify/delete ANY store in the company (incl. reassigning a store's region_id) with no region scoping. Minor intra-role over-reach.

USING/WITH CHECK = (company_id = app_company_id()) with NO role gate. Any company member (incl. supplier, store_manager) can INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE supplier escalations — a supplier could delete escalations raised against itself.

USING/WITH CHECK = (company_id = app_company_id()) with NO role gate; any company member can CRUD invites. Paired 'supplier_invites read' (company_id only) exposes the secret invite `token` (schema.sql:485) company-wide, enabling onboarding hijack / forged invites by non-admins.
Symmetric USING/WITH CHECK = company + role IN (system_admin, regional_manager, executive). Correctly gated. No issue.

WITH CHECK = app_can_see_ticket(ticket_id) (includes company scope). Anyone who can see the ticket may attach evidence; uploaded_by is not bound to auth.uid(), so the uploader can be spoofed. Low impact.

WITH CHECK = app_can_see_ticket(ticket_id). author_id and author_role are not constrained to auth.uid()/the caller's role, so a viewer can post a ticket update impersonating another user or role in the comment/audit thread.

USING/WITH CHECK = (company_id = app_company_id()) with NO role gate. Any company member can CRUD variation orders (scope/cost changes), including a store_manager or supplier fabricating cost variations.
WITH CHECK = company + (role IN (system_admin,executive,regional_manager) OR store_id IN app_store_ids()). Properly role/store scoped. No issue.

No explicit WITH CHECK; defaults to USING = company + (company-wide OR region IN app_region_ids() OR supplier IN app_supplier_ids()). store_manager is intentionally excluded. Because the new row is re-checked against the caller's own region/supplier sets, cross-scope escape is prevented; relying on the implicit check is fragile — add an explicit WITH CHECK.

CRITICAL: FOR UPDATE USING (id = auth.uid()) with NO WITH CHECK. Default WITH CHECK = USING pins only `id`; `role` and `company_id` are freely writable. With table-wide UPDATE granted to authenticated and no BEFORE-UPDATE trigger, any authenticated user can PATCH their own row to role='system_admin' (and/or company_id=<victim tenant>) via PostgREST — full privilege escalation / account & tenant takeover, since app_role()/app_is_company_wide() read this column.

WITH CHECK = (bucket_id = X AND auth.uid() IS NOT NULL) only — no per-user/company path scoping. Any authenticated user could upload arbitrary objects to any private bucket. Documented as inert (storage RLS lacks JWT claims on this migrated project → browser uploads 403 and go through the service-role /api/uploads route), but the policy is a fragile safety net if JWT propagation is ever fixed.
Symmetric USING/WITH CHECK = company + role = system_admin. Correctly gated. No issue.
Symmetric USING/WITH CHECK = company + role = system_admin. Correctly gated. No issue.
Symmetric USING/WITH CHECK = company + role = system_admin. Correctly gated. No issue.
Symmetric USING/WITH CHECK = company + role = system_admin. Internal notes admin-only. Correctly gated. No issue.

WITH CHECK = (bucket_id='project-files' AND auth.uid() IS NOT NULL). Any authenticated user could upload to project-files even though the project_files TABLE writes are system_admin-only — inconsistent, but inert per the same storage-JWT limitation. Low.

USING/WITH CHECK = (app_role() = 'system_admin') only. Table has no company_id column (global single-row-per-key). A system_admin of ANY tenant can overwrite global platform branding/settings affecting all tenants. Harmless in the current single-company deployment; a cross-tenant hazard if multiple companies each have a system_admin.

No WITH CHECK; USING allows any assigned supplier (supplier_id IN app_supplier_ids) or region RM to write ANY column — a supplier can set status='completed'/completed_at and rewrite quote_value directly, self-signing-off their own work. No app code updates tickets via the RLS-bound client, so the policy is pure attack surface.

FOR ALL (insert/update/delete) to any user who can see the ticket (store_manager, same-company supplier). WITH CHECK is company_id-only — no role or workflow-state guard, so completion certificates can be forged, altered or deleted directly.
No WITH CHECK; any see-ticket user (store_manager / same-company supplier) can UPDATE quotes.amount and status directly, tampering with the agreed price after submission.
FOR ALL scoped only by company_id — any company user incl. store_manager can insert/update/delete variation orders and set status='approved' with no role check.
Insert allowed by any see-ticket user with no workflow-state guard, so completion evidence can be added on a terminal (completed) ticket.

USING correctly restricts writes to company-wide roles or regional_manager (suppliers/store managers blocked), but WITH CHECK is company_id-only, so an in-company RM could in principle write snag rows outside their own region. Low impact.

Correctly restricted to system_admin (with check company_id + role='system_admin'); RM/executive read-only. No client can set milestone timestamps — progress_percentage is a STORED generated column. No issue.
